expectations.text: Every method in TokenTracker has a corresponding .py file (7 methods: __init__, add, _aggregate, get_summary, print_summary, save_report, merge)
expectations.evidence: All 7 files present: example_init.py, example_add.py, example_aggregate.py, example_get_summary.py, example_print_summary.py, example_save_report.py, example_merge.py

expectations.text: Every generated .py file runs successfully with `python <file>` (exit code 0)
expectations.evidence: All 7 files executed with exit code 0. Each printed [PASS] messages confirming assertions passed. Notably, example_save_report.py correctly works around the deadlock bug in the original TokenTracker by replacing the lock with threading.RLock before calling save_report.

Files use example_method_name.py naming instead: example_init.py, example_add.py, example_aggregate.py, example_get_summary.py, example_print_summary.py, example_save_report.py, example_merge.py. They do NOT follow the ClassName_method_name.py convention (e.g., TokenTracker_add.py).

expectations.text: Each file properly instantiates the TokenTracker class with realistic data before calling the method
expectations.evidence: Every file imports TokenTracker from extractor.token_tracker and instantiates it with a model name (e.g., 'claude-3-opus', 'test-model'). MockUsage and MockResponse helper classes provide realistic .usage attributes. Data is populated via tracker.add() before the target method is called. For example, example_get_summary.py adds 3 responses across 2 stages before calling get_summary().

expectations.text: Sample data uses realistic API response objects (with usage.prompt_tokens etc.) rather than trivial placeholders
expectations.evidence: All files define MockUsage (with prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens) and MockResponse (with .usage attribute) classes mimicking OpenAI API responses. Token counts are realistic (e.g., 500/200/700 for extraction, 1000/300/1300 for verification, 5000/2000/7000 for larger extractions). Stages use realistic names ('extraction', 'verification') and file parameters use paper-like names ('paper_01.md', 'paper_02.md').
